questions.text: In which of the following growth phases, the number of bacteria increases the most rapidly?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is a phase of no growth. Bacteria prepare themselves for division.
The exponential or the log phase is the phase during which the bacterium increases the most rapidly.
Bacterial death rate is equal to the bacterial rate of reproduction and multiplication in this phase.
Bacteria start dying in this phase. The death rate is more than the reproduction rate.

questions.explanation: The exponential or the log phase is the phase during which the bacterium increases the most rapidly as shown in the figure below:

questions.text: It is the smallest eukaryote:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Plasmodium belongs to the kingdom Protozoa which are Eukaryotes and are microscopic. Viruses' classification to even living things is in debate. Bacteria are prokaryotes. Sponges are found underwater and are visible by the naked eye.

questions.text: Which of the following is found in all viruses?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cell membranes are only found in eukaryotic cells.
Membrane-bound organelles and mitochondrial DNA are only found in eukaryotic cells.
Membrane-bound organelles and mitochondrial DNA are only found in eukaryotic cells.
Membrane-bound organelles and mitochondrial DNA are only found in eukaryotic cells.

questions.explanation: All viruses possess a protein coat, also known as a capsid, which encloses their genetic material. The protein coat provides protection to the viral genome and helps in the attachment of the virus to host cells. The composition and structure of the protein coat can vary among different viruses.

questions.text: Viruses are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Always viewed with a light microscope
options.explanation: The word virus is derived from Latin word venome meaning poisonous fluid. It can be deined as non cellular infectious entities which contain either RNA or DNA, normally encased in proteinaceous coat, and reproduce only in living cells.

The word virus is derived from Latin word venome meaning poisonous fluid. It can be deined as non cellular infectious entities which contain either RNA or DNA, normally encased in proteinaceous coat, and reproduce only in living cells.

This option is correct because Viruses cannot be grown on artificial media. They can reproduce only in animal and plant cells or in microorganisms, where they reproduce by replication (a process by which many copies or replicas of viruses are formed). Thus the viruses are obligate intracellular parasites.

The word virus is derived from Latin word venome meaning poisonous fluid. It can be deined as non cellular infectious entities which contain either RNA or DNA, normally encased in proteinaceous coat, and reproduce only in living cells.
Viruses lack metabolic machinery for the synthesis of their own nucleic acid and protein. They depend on the host cell to carry out these vital functions.

questions.explanation: Viruses are obligate, intracellular parasites, which means they require a host cell to reproduce and carry out their life cycle. They cannot replicate or metabolize independently outside of a host cell. Viruses are composed of genetic material (DNA or RNA) enclosed in a protein coat called capsid.

questions.text: Which of the following groups of organisms lacks motility?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellular slime molds, a.k.a. sorocarpic amoebae (Brown et al., 2011; Brown and Silberman, 2012; Spiegel et al., 2004; Olive, 1975), are amoeboid organisms in which individual amoebae, upon starvation or some other signal, aggregate to form a multicellular fruiting body, or sorocarp, containing walled, dormant spores.
Ciliates are single-celled organisms that, at some stage in their life cycle, possess cilia, short hairlike organelles used for locomotion and food gathering.

The dinoflagellates are a monophyletic group of single-celled eukaryotes constituting the phylum Dinoflagellata and are usually considered protists. Dinoflagellates are mostly marine plankton, but they also are common in freshwater habitats.

Euglenids are one of the best-known groups of flagellates, which are excavate eukaryotes of the phylum Euglenophyta and their cell structure is typical of that group. They are commonly found in freshwater, especially when it is rich in organic materials, with a few marine and endosymbiotic members.

The Apicomplexa are a large phylum of mainly parasitic alveolates. Most of them possess a unique form of organelle that comprises a type of non-photosynthetic plastid called an apicoplast, and an apical complex structure. The organelle is an adaptation that the apicomplexan applies in penetration of a host cell.

questions.explanation: Apicomplexans have no means of motility. They are parasites that are transferred among hosts by the activities of the hosts.

questions.text: Trypanosoma belongs to the class:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sarcodina is a class of protozoa that includes amoebas. Amoebas are characterized by their ability to move and feed using pseudopodia, which are temporary extensions of their cytoplasm. They typically live in freshwater environments and have a flexible cell membrane that allows them to change shape.

Flagellata is a class of protozoa that possess flagella, which are whip-like structures used for movement. These organisms use their flagella to propel themselves through their environment. They are typically found in aquatic environments, including freshwater and marine habitats.

Ciliata is a class of protozoa that have cilia, which are hair-like structures used for locomotion and feeding. Ciliates use the coordinated beating of their cilia to move through their environment. They are often found in freshwater habitats and are known for their complex cell structures and diverse feeding methods.

Suctoria is a class of protozoa that are parasitic and possess specialized structures called suctoria that they use to attach to and feed on other organisms. Suctoria have a stalk-like structure with a bulbous head containing tentacles or sucking tubes used to extract nutrients from their hosts. They are typically found in marine environments and are considered parasites of other organisms.

Sporozoa is a class of protozoa that are characterized by their ability to produce spores. They are typically parasitic and have complex life cycles involving multiple hosts. Sporozoans are often responsible for diseases in animals, including humans, such as malaria (caused by Plasmodium) and toxoplasmosis (caused by Toxoplasma gondii).

questions.explanation: The flagellates are generally considered to be the basic stock from which evolved not only other kinds of protozoa but also higher plants and animals e.g. Trypanosoma.

questions.text: All of the following organisms belong to the kingdom Protista EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ulva is a type of algae that is commonly found in marine environments. It is classified as a green alga, which is a type of photosynthetic protist.

Euglena is a type of protozoan that is characterized by its flagellum, which it uses to move around. It can be both photosynthetic and heterotrophic, meaning that it can produce its own food through photosynthesis or obtain food from other organisms.
Slime Mold is a type of protist that is characterized by its amoeboid form and its ability to form multicellular colonies. Slime molds are decomposers, meaning that they feed on dead organic matter.
Common molds belong to kingdom fungi, not kingdom protista. While all other options are examples of kingdom protista.
Suctoria is a type of ciliate that is parasitic on other organisms. Suctoria are characterized by their tentacles, which they use to capture and consume their prey.

questions.explanation: Common Molds belong to the Kingdom Fungi.

questions.text: The disease caused by Protozoal infection is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Rickets is caused by vitamin D deficiency.
Goiter is caused by iron deficiency.

Malaria is caused by protozoa of the genus Plasmodium. Four species cause disease in humans: P falciparum, P vivax, P ovale, and P malariae. Malaria is a vector-borne infectious disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. The parasites are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. Hence, this option is correct.
Beri-beri is a vitamin B1 deficiency disease that causes neurological and cardiovascular problems.
Alzeheimer is a non-pathogenic, inherited disorder.

questions.explanation: Malaria is a vector-borne infectious disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. The parasites are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. Malaria is a major public health problem in many parts of the world, particularly in Africa, Asia, and South America.

questions.text: Which of the following is divided by fission?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses are not divided by fission. They reproduce by infecting host cells and using their host's metabolic machinery to replicate.
Viroids are survival agents that are composed of a single strand of RNA and lack the protein coat of a virus. They also don't undergo fission.
Out of the given options, only bacteria undergo binary fission. Binary fission is a type of asexual reproduction in which a single cell divides into two identical daughter cells.
Fungi reproduce via spores or sexual reproduction and not by fission.

questions.explanation: Binary fission is a type of asexual reproduction in which a single cell divides into two identical daughter cells. The process begins with the replication of the bacterial chromosome, followed by the formation of a septum that divides the cell into two equal halves. Finally, the septum breaks down, and the two daughter cells are released.

questions.text: Red algae do not contribute towards:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Red algae are essential components of coral reef ecosystems, providing the foundational structure and calcium carbonate for reef growth. Their ability to secrete calcium carbonate helps bind coral colonies together, forming the intricate reef structures that support a diverse range of marine life.

Red algae contribute to limestone formation through their calcium carbonate deposition. Over time, the accumulation of calcium carbonate from red algae and other marine organisms can form extensive limestone deposits, which serve as a valuable geological resource.

This option is correct because this is not the practical importance of Red algae. Red algae play a crucial role in marine ecosystems and contribute to the formation of coral reefs, limestone deposits, and chalk deposits. However, their contribution to fertilizer production is negligible.

Chalk is a type of soft, white limestone composed primarily of the remains of microscopic algae, including red algae. The calcium carbonate secreted by red algae plays a significant role in the formation of chalk deposits, which are used in various applications, including cement production and writing materials.

questions.explanation: The multicellular body form of red algae is commonly composed of complex interwoven filaments that are delicate and feathery. A few red algae are flattened sheets of cells. Most multicellular red algae attach to rocks or other substances by a basal holdfast. Some red algae incorporate calcium carbonate in their cell walls from the ocean and take part in building coral reefs along with coral animals. Red algae do not contribute to making fertilizers.

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE about Amoebae?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebae lack lagella and move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia (false feet).
Amoebae are Unicellular with no definite shape.

options.text: They do not cause any disease in humans
options.explanation: The intestinal parasite, Entamoeba histolytica, causes amoebic dysentery in humans.

options.text: They move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia
options.explanation: This option is correct because Amoebae lack lagella and move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia (false feet).

questions.explanation: Amoeba moves from one place to another using their cytoplasmic processes and by the process of pseudopodia. Amoeba are single-celled (unicellular organisms), not possessing flagella (as shown in the diagram below). They can cause various diseases in humans such as amoebiasis, which is a form of dysentery.

questions.text: A student studied a drop of pond water with the low power of compound microscope and made the following exact drawing of an organism she observed, Which kingdom is the organism classified as?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Kingdom protista has membrane bound organelles as shown in the diagram and a true nucleus. That is why, option A is correct.
Option B is wrong because organisms belonging to Kingdom Monera cannot have a true nucleus and membrane bound organelles.
Option C is wrong because it is not a kingdom rather a subclassification of Kingdom Animalia.
Option D is wrong because the organism shown is unicellular.
Option E is wrong because bryophyta is not a kingdom but a subclassification of plant kingdom.

questions.explanation: Kingdom protista has membrane bound organelles as shown in the diagram and a true nucleus. That is why, option A is correct.

questions.text: The gametophyte of Ulva is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A is the correct option because Ulva cycles between two morphologically similar multicellular adult phases, a haploid gametophyte, and a diploid sporophyte. Diploid sporophytes produce haploid zoospores that develop into gametophytes. Haploid gametophytes produce haploid gametes.
Option B is wrong because sporophyte of ulva is diploid, Its gametophyte is haploid.
Option C is wrong because it is a fact as shown in life cycle of Ulva.
Option D is wrong because it is a fact as shown in life cycle of Ulva.

questions.explanation: Ulva cycles between two morphologically similar multicellular adult phases, a haploid gametophyte, and a diploid sporophyte. Diploid sporophytes produce haploid zoospores that develop into gametophytes. Haploid gametophytes produce haploid gametes.

questions.text: Symptoms of malaria occur specifically due to the formation of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the form of the malaria parasite that is injected into the human body by a female Anopheles mosquito during a blood meal. Sporozoites travel through the bloodstream and eventually enter liver cells, where they multiply and differentiate into merozoites.

Symptoms of malaria occur specifically due to the formation of merozoites. Merozoites are a form of the malaria parasite that infects red blood cells, leading to their destruction and causing the characteristic symptoms of the disease, such as fever, chills, headache, and fatigue. The release of merozoites from infected red blood cells triggers an immune response, which can also contribute to the severity of symptoms. Hence, this option is correct.

This is the form of the malaria parasite that is produced by the asexual replication of merozoites within human red blood cells. Gamenocytes are not involved in causing symptoms of malaria, but they are responsible for transmitting the disease from humans to mosquitoes during a blood meal.

This term usually refers to the female gamete (egg cell) produced by animals, including humans. In the context of malaria, there is no such thing as an oocyte, as the parasite responsible for the disease is not an animal and does not have gametes in the traditional sense.

questions.explanation: The fever and chills of malaria are associated with the rupture of erythrocytic-stage schizonts. In severe falciparum malaria, parasitized red cells may obstruct capillaries and postcapillary venules, leading to local hypoxia and the release of toxic cellular products. The invasive blood stage of malaria parasites, merozoites, are complex entities specialized for the capture and entry of red blood cells.

questions.text: Select a parasitic zooflagellate:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trichonympha are complex, specialized flagellates with many flagella which live as symbionts in the guts of termites and help in dthe igestion of dry wood.
Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing African sleeping sickness. It is transmitted by the bite of infected tse-tse fly. Hence, this option is correct.

Choanoflagellates are sessile marine or freshwater flagellates which are attached by a stalk and their single flagellum is surrounded by a delicate collar. They are of special interest because of their striking resemblance to collar cells in sponges.
Euglena is a genus of single cell flagellate eukaryotes. It is found in freshwater sources like a quiet puddle or pond and can even be found in a swimming pool.

questions.explanation: Protozoa that move by means of flagella are called zooflagellates. Trypanosoma is a human parasitic flagellate. It is transmitted by the bite of a tsetse (se-se) fly and it is the cause of African sleeping sickness.

questions.text: Chlorophyta is considered to be closest to plants but do not resemble plants in having:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall.
Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall.
Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall.

Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall, it differs in the structure of its sex organs. Hence, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: Chlorophyta is a phylum of green algae that live in marine habitats. Some of them are found in freshwater and on land. Some species have even become adapted to thriving in extreme environments, such as deserts, arctic regions, and hypersaline habitats. The predominant pigment is chlorophyll (particularly, a and b). The storage form in Chlorophyta is starch and the cell wall is comprised of Cellulose cell wall.

questions.text: Freshwater protozoans pump out excess water by a special structure called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The oral groove is a structure found in certain protozoans, such as paramecia, which is involved in the intake of food particles through ciliary movement.
This option is correct. Freshwater protozoans have a contractile vacuole, which is a specialized organelle responsible for regulating water balance within the cell.
This option is incorrect. While vesicles are membrane-bound sacs involved in various cellular processes, they are not specifically associated with pumping out excess water in freshwater protozoans.
This option is incorrect. Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles found in cells that have various functions, such as storing substances, maintaining turgor pressure, and facilitating digestion.

questions.explanation: The organelle used to remove excess water in protozoa is a contractile vacuole. Water is able to move through the cellular membrane of protozoa by osmosis.

questions.text: These protists are mostly unicellular (a few are colonial) organisms with spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus. They possess from one to many long, whip-like flagella that enable them to move. Flagellates move rapidly, pulling themselves forward by lashing flexible flagella that are usually located at the anterior end. Examples include Trypanosome, Euglena, etc. The above give lines indicate which of the following groups of protozoa:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebas are not characterized by possessing whip-like flagella. They typically move using pseudopodia, which are temporary extensions of their cytoplasm.
Arthropods are a completely different group of organisms, which includes insects, spiders, and crustaceans. They are not unicellular or protozoa.

Foraminifera are marine protozoa characterized by the presence of a shell, often with intricate patterns. They move using pseudopodia and do not possess whip-like flagella as described in the given lines.

The characteristics described, including being mostly unicellular (with some colonial forms), having spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus, and possessing whip-like flagella for rapid movement, match the group of protozoa called zooflagellates.

questions.explanation: Zooflagellates are mostly unicellular (a few are colonial) organisms with spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus. They possess from one to many long, whip-like flagella that enable them to move. Flagellates move rapidly, pulling themselves forward by lashing flexible lagella, that are usually located at the anterior end. Flagellates obtain their food either by ingesting living or dead organisms or by absorbing nutrients from dead or decomposing organic matter. They may be free-living, symbionts or parasites. Trichonymphas are complex, specialized flagellates with many flagella which live as symbionts in the guts of termites and help in the digestion of dry wood.

questions.text: All of the following are the characteristics of the kingdom Protista EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This statement is true.
Members of the kingdom Protista can be free living is a true statement.
Some members of kingdom Protista move via flagella is a genuine statement regarding kingdom Protista.

options.text: Some members are shaped like rods and termed bacilli
options.explanation: Protists are a group of loosely connected, mostly unicellular eukaryotic organisms that are not plants, animals, or fungi. Among the given characteristics, rod-shaped cells called "Bacilli" are an exclusive classification of bacteria. Hence, this option is correct.

options.text: Some members spend part of their life cycle inside insects
options.explanation: Some members of kingdom protista spend part of their life cycle inside insects is a true statement.

questions.explanation: Protists are a group of loosely connected, mostly unicellular eukaryotic organisms that are not plants, animals, or fungi. There is no single feature such as evolutionary history or morphology common to all these organisms and they are unofficially placed under a separate kingdom called Protista.

questions.text: Which of the following results from conjugation in Paramecium?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Conjugation is a method of sexual reproduction. Hence, it cannot cause death. So, this option is incorrect.
As conjugation leads to cell division in a paramecium zygote, this is the correct option.
Budding is a method of asexual reproduction, mostly observed in yeast. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Recombination means the exchange of genetic material. It does occur during conjugation. However, it is a step of conjugation and not a result. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The process of sexual reproduction in paramecium is termed conjugation. During conjugation, two sexually opposite paramecium mate and exchange their hereditary material. At last, it results in the formation of a diploid zygote. The zygote becomes the new paramecium by meiosis. Hence, cell division is the result of conjugation in paramecium.

questions.text: This protist has isomorphic alternation of generation:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phytophthora is a genus of oomycetes, which are fungus-like organisms that are closely related to algae. Phytophthora species are plant pathogens that can cause a variety of diseases, including late blight of potatoes, sudden oak death, and root rot of citrus trees.

It is fresh water algae found floating in stagnant water of ponds, ditches etc. It is easily cultured and has been used as an experimental in research on photosynthesis as well as being investigated as an alternate of food.

This option is correct because Ulva exhibits isomorphic alternation of generation. Both the haploid and diploid stages of Ulva are morphologically similar, appearing as flat, sheet-like structures. This contrasts with heteromorphic alternation of generation, where the haploid and diploid stages have distinct morphologies.

Euglena is typical of protoctists that have both animal-like and plant-like characteristics. A very long flagellum propels the body, which is enveloped by a flexible pellicle made of protein. A photoreceptor allows Euglena to find light, after which photosynthesis can occur in its numerous chloroplasts. In addition to the pyrenoids, which store starch, there are starch granules in the cytoplasm.

questions.explanation: Ulva, also called sea lettuce, exists in two forms: 26 chromosomal state (sporophyte) and 13 chromosomal state (gametophyte). Since both of these states are morphologically identical to each other, Ulva shows isomorphic alternation of generation

questions.text: Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is correct since Trypanosoma is a parasite that causes African sleeping sickness that is transmitted by the tsetse fly.
Option B is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause European sleeping sickness. The term "European sleeping sickness" is not a recognized medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites.
Option C is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause Indonesian sleeping sickness. The term "Indonesian sleeping sickness" is not a recognized medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites.
Option D is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause American sleeping sickness. The term "American sleeping sickness" is not a recognized medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites.

questions.explanation: Parasitic flagellates cause diseases. For example Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing African sleeping sickness. It is transmitted by the bite of infected tsetse fly.

questions.text: The feeding stage of slime mold is a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because Gastrozooid is feeding stage in cnidaria.
Option B is incorrect because Sporozoite is a motile spore-like stage in the malarial parasite.

Option C is correct since The feeding stage of a slime mold is a plasmodium, a multinucleate mass of cytoplasm that can grow to 30 cm (1 ft) in diameter. The plasmodium, which is slimy in appearance, streams over damp, decaying logs and leaf litter. It often forms a network of channels that cover a large surface area. As it creeps along, it ingests bacteria, yeasts, spores and decaying organic matter
Option D is incorrect because Merozoite is the stage of malarial parasites that infect red blood cells.

questions.explanation: Usually, plasmodial slime molds exist as a plasmodium. It is a diploid multinucleated cytoplasmic mass enveloped by a slime sheath. At times unfavorable to growth, such as during drought the Plasmodium develops many sporangia.

questions.text: Sleeping sickness in humans is caused by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sleeping sickness, also known as African trypanosomiasis, is caused by various species of Trypanosoma parasites, with Trypanosoma brucei being the primary causative agent. It is transmitted to humans through the bite of infected tsetse flies.
Anopheles mosquitoes are vectors for the transmission of Plasmodium parasites, which cause malaria. Anopheles mosquitoes are not associated with the transmission of Trypanosoma or sleeping sickness.
Plasmodium parasites are responsible for causing malaria in humans. Malaria is a different disease from sleeping sickness and is transmitted by infected Anopheles mosquitoes.
The Andes is a mountain range in South America and is not associated with the transmission of sleeping sickness or any of the mentioned parasites.

questions.explanation: Sleeping sickness, also known as African trypanosomiasis, is caused by various species of Trypanosoma parasites, with Trypanosoma brucei being the primary causative agent. It is transmitted to humans through the bite of infected tse tse flies.
